# GDPR Compliance Guide (Internal)

An internal working checklist for staying compliant with UK GDPR — the public-facing explanation of the same rules lives on the site's [Privacy Policy](/privacy-policy.html) and [Data Processing Notice](/data-processing-notice.html); this document is the "have we actually done it" version.

## The core principles, checked against current practice

| Principle | How Aegis meets it today |
|---|---|
| Lawfulness, fairness, transparency | Privacy Policy and Data Processing Notice published and linked from every page footer |
| Purpose limitation | Enquiry data used only to respond to enquiries and deliver engaged work — not repurposed |
| Data minimisation | Enquiry form only collects fields actually needed to scope a project (see the form itself for the exact list) |
| Accuracy | Client can request correction at any time — see Data Processing Notice |
| Storage limitation | See [`DataRetentionPolicy.md`](DataRetentionPolicy.md) for defined periods per data category |
| Integrity and confidentiality (security) | See [`SecurityPolicy.md`](SecurityPolicy.md) and [`Security.md`](../Security.md) |
| Accountability | This document, plus the retention/security policies, is the evidence of accountability — keep them current, not just written once |

## When a Data Processing Agreement is needed
Any time a project involves Aegis processing a **client's own customers'** personal data (not just Aegis's own enquiry/client data) — use [`DataProcessingAgreement.md`](DataProcessingAgreement.md) for that engagement. Check this at the proposal stage, not after the project starts.

## Handling a data subject rights request
1. Confirm the requester's identity is reasonably established (don't hand over data to whoever emails claiming to be someone).
2. Identify what data is actually held about them (enquiry records, project files, email threads).
3. Respond within a reasonable time — UK GDPR's statutory maximum is one month, but for a company this size, faster and more personal is both possible and better for trust.
4. Log that the request was handled (what was requested, what was done) — this is your accountability evidence if ever questioned.

## Reporting a breach
See [`IncidentResponsePlan.md`](IncidentResponsePlan.md) — the 72-hour ICO notification window is the single most time-sensitive compliance obligation in UK GDPR; don't let uncertainty about severity delay considering it.

## Annual review
Revisit this guide, the retention policy, and the security policy at least once a year, or immediately after any change to what data is collected or how it's processed (a new productized AI line, a new integration, a new sub-processor).
